note: Erik 2026-07-07: when using skills and having skills used against you, there should sometimes be a SKILL BATTLE — the types of skills used, the energy applied, etc. all determine the effect, resolving over several turns. This is contested skill-vs-skill: not one roll, but an exchange where matchup + investment + tier decide who prevails. Builds on the d100 resolution, intensity_scaling (energy), challengeProfile (matchup), and the social-contest structure (SNG-027 is the SOCIAL flavor of this). Authored by Aevi (PO).

whenItTriggers.principle: Not every skill use is a battle. A skill-battle happens when two wills contest the SAME outcome with skills — my Umbracraft conceal vs your Radiance reveal; my Foreclose vs your Hold-the-Aperture; my the-Edge strike vs your the-Stand shield; my Falsecraft lie vs your Verity. When one side acts and the other RESISTS with a skill (rather than just taking it), it's a battle.
whenItTriggers.vsSingleCheck: A skill used against the unresisting WORLD is a single check (pick a lock, cross a river). A skill used against a RESISTING will that answers with its own skill escalates to a battle. The GM (or engine) flags the contest when both sides commit skills to opposite outcomes.
whenItTriggers.duration: Short battles resolve in one exchange (one skill each, compare). Bigger ones run MULTIPLE ROUNDS — a back-and-forth where each round shifts a momentum meter until one side breaks, yields, or achieves their outcome. Tier and stakes set the length: a Tier-V clash of masters is a several-round set-piece.

resolution.theExchange: Each round, both sides declare a skill + an INTENSITY (energy poured in: Conserve 0.6x / Standard / Surge 1.6x, per intensity_scaling). Each rolls d100 + attribute + ability-tier bonus + MATCHUP bonus. Higher net wins the round and shifts the momentum meter toward their outcome by the margin.
resolution.matchup: The core of it — skills are strong/weak against each other by FUNCTION and DOMAIN (like the pole relationships). A conceal vs a reveal: reveal has the edge (light finds shadow) unless the concealer out-tiers or out-spends. A strike vs a shield: shield blunts but doesn't win; a strike vs a bind: the bound striker is at disadvantage. The matchup table (below) gives a bonus/penalty; being the RIGHT tool for the contest is worth more than raw rolls.
resolution.energyEconomy: Intensity is the gamble: Surge (1.6x) adds power but drains energy fast and risks backlash (25% by tier); Conserve (0.6x) is sustainable but weak. A skill-battle is also a WAR OF ATTRITION — whoever runs out of energy first loses the ability to contest, even if winning on rolls. Managing the energy curve across rounds IS the skill of skill-battling. A master who Surges every round wins fast or collapses.
resolution.tierWeight: Higher-tier skills hit harder in the matchup (a Tier-V ability brings a large flat edge) AND have the stamina to sustain more rounds (their epic proficiency means fewer wasted exchanges). This is why tier matters: not just bigger numbers, but the ability to WIN or OUTLAST across a multi-round contest.
resolution.momentumMeter: A -X..+X meter; each round's winner pushes it by their margin. Reaching one end = that side achieves their outcome (the truth revealed, the escape made, the lie held, the strike landed decisively). A big enough single-round win (a Surged Tier-V crit) can end it in one blow; evenly matched wills grind across rounds.
resolution.outcomes: Win (your outcome happens), Break (opponent out of energy/yields — your outcome + they're spent), Stalemate (both exhausted, neither prevails, the situation resolves by fiction), Backlash-loss (you Surged and botched — your outcome fails AND you take the backlash).

matchupTable.note: Function-vs-function edges in a contest. +2 strong edge, +1 slight, 0 neutral, defensive functions BLUNT rather than beat. Domain/pole relationships (cofiring, opposition) modify further. The GM/engine reads this to set each round's matchup bonus.

edges.result: +2 reveal — light finds shadow (unless concealer out-tiers/out-spends)
-2 — but a Tier gap or Surge can flip it; the Never-There beats ordinary reveal
0 — shield BLUNTS (reduces margin), doesn't win; sustained striking wears a shield down
+1 attacker if free; -2 if the striker is the one bound
+1 break — unmaking beats holding, but a Tier-V ward (hold_the_aperture) resists
+2 bind — you can't flee what holds you (unless move out-tiers)
-2 — but Break-Away/Pathless-Way can slip a lesser bind
+2 — Verity vs Falsecraft; the Whole Truth beats even the true-pieces lie
attrition — heal doesn't beat a strike but OUTLASTS it (Rooted Stand); a war of endurance
pure contest — tier + energy + roll decide; two concealers, two strikers: the better-invested wins

matchupTable.domainModifiers: Opposed poles clash HARDER (bigger swings, faster resolution): life vs death, order vs chaos, light vs dark contesting is volatile. Co-firing/adjacent poles interact oddly (a life-user vs a death-user at the Quiet-in-the-Quickwood fight on strange ground). The GM applies a situational modifier from the location's disposition too — fighting a shadow-battle in the Radiant Wastes favors the light.

examples.battle: Umbral infiltrator (the Never-There, T5) vs a Radiant warden (Radiance, T2)
examples.how: conceal vs reveal = -2 base for the concealer, BUT the T5-vs-T2 gap (+large) and a Surge flip it. Round 1 the warden's Radiance sweeps (reveal edge), infiltrator Surges the Never-There to erase the trace faster than light maps it. 2-3 rounds; if the infiltrator's energy holds, the warden is left staring at an empty room. If it runs out, the light catches the afterimage.

the signature skill-battle. Each round the liar layers falsehood, the reader peels it. Verity has the +2 truth-vs-falsehood edge, so the liar must out-tier or out-spend. A master liar (Woven Untruth, T3) vs an apprentice reader can hold; the Whole Truth (T5) cracks anything. Momentum = how much of the lie stands when the dust settles.

examples.battle: the Edge duelist (Last Form, T5) vs a Churn-reaver (Unraveling Blow, T3)
examples.how: strike vs strike, but the reaver's CHAOS domain resists the Edge's ORDER-read — the Last Form needs a form to read, and chaos gives it none (-matchup). The duelist's tier edge vs the reaver's matchup edge: a real contest, several rounds, decided by who commits energy at the right moment.

buildImplication: Engine: a skill-battle mode alongside single-check resolution — triggered when two wills contest with skills. Per round: both declare skill+intensity; roll d100+attr+tierBonus+matchupBonus; winner shifts momentum by margin; track energy (attrition); resolve at meter-end or exhaustion. Reads challengeProfile (matchup relevance), functions (the edge table), intensity_scaling (energy), tier (weight). SNG-027 social contest is the SOCIAL-flavored instance of this same engine — unify them: a skill-battle is the general case, social-contest and combat and the duel are flavors. Matchup table authored here; expand per-function. Flag SNG-045. Smoke: a conceal-vs-reveal contest runs multiple rounds; energy attrition can decide it independent of rolls; tier gives both power and stamina; a Surge-botch backlashes; matchup edge beats raw rolls; social-contest resolves through the same engine.
